tasks.instruction: Create a spam filtering policy for external emails whose subject contains specified marketing keywords like “Sale” or “Discount,” set its sensitivity to a medium-high level, and move matching messages into the mail folder designated for marketing and promotional emails.

tasks.instruction: Create an alert rule that sends an email notification to the security administrator whenever the count of quarantined messages becomes greater than 100 within any 1-hour window.

tasks.instruction: From recent email activity, add the domains of all external messages that were successfully delivered with very low spam scores to the allowed list, and add the sender address of any external message that was blocked as spam to the blocked list.

tasks.instruction: Configure a scheduled report that emails a daily summary of quarantined messages at 6:00 PM in HTML format to the mailbox belonging to the IT manager, using a clear descriptive report name.

tasks.instruction: Create an attachment control rule for external senders that blocks any message with an attachment larger than 15 MB and sends the recipient a notification containing a brief explanation of the size limit.

expected_values.notification_message: Attachment blocked due to size limit

tasks.instruction: Using email logs from the last 7 days, identify the external sender domain with the highest number of messages blocked due to malware and add that domain to the blocked domains list.

tasks.instruction: Create a temporary allow rule that, for 24 hours, lets messages from a specified external domain bypass spam filtering (while still being scanned for malware) for only the mailbox used by the QA tester.
